Confidential Incident Report (Hong Kong) — Subject to SFO Part XIVA and HKEX Listing Rules; internal circulation only.
Qing Harbour Holdings Limited (HKEX-listed) reports a cyber incident identified at 02:10 HKT involving our vendor Orchid Digital Custody (HK) Limited supporting the Group’s virtual-asset settlement pilot for ESG-linked procurement.
Initial forensics indicate unauthorised access to the vendor’s offsite backup; an HSM key shard and encrypted API tokens may have been copied, though no on‑chain transfers from the pilot treasury wallet have been observed.
Our integration server mirrored certain folders; logs suggest potential exfiltration of ESG procurement files and an M&A working deck, including a draft term sheet for the proposed acquisition of Willow Peak Renewables Limited with an indicative consideration of HKD 2.6–2.9 billion, earn‑out triggers, and a target announcement window; this constitutes inside information.
We have invoked the inside information protocol, created an Insider List (Mr. Adrian Lau, CFO; Ms. Carmen Yip, Head of Treasury; Mr. Felix Chow, CISO; Ms. Novie Lin, Legal Counsel), and implemented an information barrier; external advisers will be wall‑crossed under NDA before access is granted.
All directors and relevant employees are reminded the Company is in the closed period before interim results; dealing is prohibited, and no tipping or selective disclosure is permitted under the SFO and HKEX Listing Rules.
Counterparty communications (including with Sapphire Ridge Capital in connection with green loan pre‑sounding) must route via the Company Secretary; do not reference the acquisition, virtual asset pilot, or commercial terms in any vendor tickets.
PDPO assessment: names, work emails, and job titles of 27 staff in ESG and Treasury may have been accessed; no HKID numbers, personal phone numbers, or residential addresses are stored in the affected folders.
Remediation actions: rotate VA wallet with new multi‑sig policy, revoke and re‑issue keys, disable vendor connectivity pending review of an SFC‑licensed alternative, and move M&A materials to a clean‑room (Room 4F) with separate credentials.
Disclosure analysis: we consider safe harbour for confidential negotiations may apply; if leakage is detected or terms crystallise, a prompt announcement will be made on HKExnews.
Operational containment includes a freeze on non‑essential system changes and a mandatory password reset for users with access to ESG and Treasury repositories by 18:00 HKT today.
The sustainability marketing team will continue general staff education on carbon accounting and wallet‑key hygiene; these materials are operational in nature and contain no investment recommendations or inside information.
For avoidance of doubt, no customer funds or external investor accounts are held in the pilot wallet; the only on‑chain balance is a test allocation of 18,500 “GreenLeaf” tokens with no external market quotation.
Contact: Ms. Janice Poon, Company Secretary (janice.poon@qingharbour.com.hk) and Incident Desk (incident@qingharbour.com.hk); escalation to Dr. Kelvin Ng, Chair of the Audit & Risk Committee, via secure channel on request.
